Politics.headline: Trump claims hostilities have ended in Iran in letter to congressional leaders
Politics.detail: President Trump seemed to suggest that the legislative deadline to approve war no longer applies as Democrats push back, signaling a possible de-escalation of tensions.

Politics.headline: Thousands in US join 'no school, no work, no shopping' May Day protest and economic blackout
Politics.detail: Across the United States, thousands participated in May Day protests, also described as an economic blackout, to voice their concerns on various political and social issues.

Politics.headline: US lawmakers say DoJ rushed Southern Poverty Law Center indictment, citing whistleblower
Politics.detail: US lawmakers are investigating claims from a whistleblower that the Department of Justice rushed the indictment of the Southern Poverty Law Center.

Politics.headline: FACT FOCUS: Why nearly 4.3 million people are no longer receiving food stamps.
Politics.detail: A fact check explains the reasons behind the decrease in the number of people receiving food stamps in the US.

Politics.headline: Debate must not become delay
Politics.detail: An article discusses how disagreements in Taiwan's political system are leading to inaction, particularly regarding the defense budget and constitutional tensions, which could undermine the country's ability to deter threats.

Finance & Economy.headline: Spirit Airlines prepares to cease operations amid financial struggles and high oil prices
Finance & Economy.detail: The company had struggled to increase post-pandemic demand before the war in Iran pushed up jet fuel costs, leading to operational challenges.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump tears up part of EU tariff deal to raise import duties on cars and lorries
Finance & Economy.detail: President Trump has decided to increase import duties on cars and lorries from the EU, reversing a previous agreement.

Finance & Economy.headline: US economic growth rebounds 2% as consumer spending slows amid Iran war
Finance & Economy.detail: The U.S. economy showed signs of recovery with a 2% growth rate, although consumer spending has slowed down due to the ongoing conflict in Iran.

Finance & Economy.headline: Bank of England joins other central banks in freezing rate cuts as Iran war upends global economy.
Finance & Economy.detail: Due to the uncertainty and economic disruption caused by the Iran war, the Bank of England has decided to hold off on cutting interest rates, aligning with other central banks.

Finance & Economy.headline: Global economy faces crossroads in wake of energy shock - JPMorgan
Finance & Economy.detail: JPMorgan's chief economist says that the global economy is at a crossroads, with energy shocks from the Middle East conflict creating a tug-of-war between economic momentum and building price pressures.

Technology.headline: Microsoft to Clean Up Windows 11's Widgets Board, Focus on 'Calm'
Technology.detail: Microsoft is planning to declutter Windows 11's Widgets board by hiding the MSN News feed and prioritizing a more minimalist and 'calm' default experience for users.

Technology.headline: Exclusive-US officials weigh cutting deadlines to fix digital flaws amid worries over AI-powered hacking, sources say
Technology.detail: U.S. cybersecurity officials are considering significantly reducing the time frame for fixing critical vulnerabilities in government IT systems, driven by concerns that hackers could exploit these flaws using AI tools. The response deadline may be reduced from two or three weeks to just three days.

Technology.headline: OpenAI Eyes AI Agent Phone, Kuo Says
Technology.detail: OpenAI is reportedly exploring the development of an AI agent smartphone, potentially shifting the focus from traditional apps to a more integrated, AI-driven mobile experience.

Technology.headline: xAI launches Grok 4.3, featuring “always-on reasoning”, 1M token context window, and low API pricing, and releases a voice cloning suite called Custom Voices
Technology.detail: xAI has launched Grok 4.3, which features always-on reasoning and a 1 million token context window, along with a voice cloning suite called Custom Voices.

Technology.headline: Spotify Rolls Out Green 'Verified' Badges to Distinguish Real Artists From AI Slop
Technology.detail: Spotify has introduced green 'Verified' badges to help users differentiate authentic artists from AI-generated content on the platform.

Science.headline: AI-Driven Future of Autonomous Science
Science.detail: An expert discusses how AI will integrate into all fields of science, changing how research is done by automating repetitive tasks and allowing scientists to focus on holistic analysis and creative problem-solving.

Science.headline: Unusual ant interaction hints at mutualistic 'cleaning' system
Science.detail: An unusual interaction between ants suggests a mutualistic 'cleaning' system.

Science.headline: MOREH Demonstrates LLM Inference on Tenstorrent Galaxy
Science.detail: MOREH validated LLM inference performance on the Tenstorrent Galaxy Wormhole system using its proprietary MoAI Inference Framework, matching or surpassing NVIDIA DGX A100-class systems.

Science.headline: Discover Mishkat World at the Mishkat Interactive Center
Science.detail: The Mishkat Interactive Center in Riyadh offers hands-on experiences and exhibits exploring energy concepts.

Science.headline: A leading journal finds that AI is flooding academic publishing with lower quality work.
Science.detail: A leading journal has discovered that academic publishing is seeing an influx of lower quality work due to AI.

Health.headline: FDA Approves First Non-Antipsychotic Drug for Alzheimer's Agitation
Health.detail: An oral NMDA receptor antagonist receives an expanded indication for treating Alzheimer's agitation, offering a new approach without the typical side effects of antipsychotics.

Health.headline: US Court Blocks Mail-order Access to Abortion Drugs, for Now
Health.detail: A U.S. court has temporarily blocked mail-order access to abortion drugs, creating uncertainty for patients seeking this option.

Health.headline: Health Canada approves 2nd generic version of Ozempic — and it's Canadian
Health.detail: Health Canada has approved a second generic version of Ozempic, which is manufactured by a Canadian company, Apotex.

Health.headline: Capoeira helps patients with Parkinson's disease in Brazil
Health.detail: Capoeira, a martial art disguised as dance, is being utilized in Brazil to assist patients with Parkinson's disease by improving their physical health through specific movements.

Health.headline: Ghana rejects US Global Health Strategy MoU
Health.detail: Ghana has rejected its bilateral memorandum of understanding with the US as part of the America First Global Health Strategy, following Zimbabwe's similar rejection.

Sports.headline: Wood's penalty gives Nottingham Forest a 1-0 win over Aston Villa in Europa League semifinal 1st leg
Sports.detail: Nottingham Forest secured a 1-0 victory over Aston Villa in the first leg of their Europa League semifinal match, thanks to a penalty from Chris Wood.

Sports.headline: Benfica's Mourinho says no contact with Real Madrid about manager's job
Sports.detail: Benfica's coach, Mourinho, has stated that there has been no contact with Real Madrid regarding the manager position.

Sports.headline: Fuel Masters trim Tropang 5G in OT behind Tio triple near the buzzer
Sports.detail: The Fuel Masters defeated Tropang 5G in overtime, with Tyler Tio hitting a crucial three-pointer at the buzzer.

Sports.headline: Leeds beat Burnley to take big step towards survival
Sports.detail: Leeds took a significant step towards Premier League survival with a win over Burnley.

Sports.headline: Rahul, Nissanka revive Capitals in style
Sports.detail: KL Rahul and Pathum Nissanka led Delhi Capitals to a 7-wicket victory over Rajasthan Royals, chasing down a target of 226.

Entertainment.headline: Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson Pulled Over by Police for Tinted Windows
Entertainment.detail: The 53-year-old actor was pulled over shortly after leaving Emily Blunt and Stanley Tucci's ceremony.

Entertainment.headline: Former One Direction star cancels most upcoming tour dates amid mystery health battle
Entertainment.detail: The singer had planned to play 20 shows across the US before he was hospitalised for an unspecified health issue.

Entertainment.headline: Kylie Jenner Sued by Second Housekeeper
Entertainment.detail: Kylie Jenner is facing another lawsuit from a former housekeeper.

Entertainment.headline: Zayn Malik Cancels Several Tour Dates
Entertainment.detail: Zayn Malik had to cancel many of his 2026 'KONNAKOL Tour' dates, including in the U.S., after he was hospitalized for an unspecified health issue.

Entertainment.headline: The Devil Wears Prada 2 in cinemas worldwide today
Entertainment.detail: The sequel to the popular movie 'The Devil Wears Prada' has been released in cinemas all over the world.

Crime & Justice.headline: Family calls for calm after riot over accused murderer in Alice Springs
Crime & Justice.detail: The family of a five-year-old girl allegedly murdered by a recently released prisoner has called for calm following a night of chaos in an outback town where an angry crowd attacked the accused and tried to storm the hospital.

Crime & Justice.headline: Clashes in Alice Springs after little girl's alleged killer arrested
Crime & Justice.detail: A recently released prisoner has been arrested after an abducted girl was found dead, sparking clashes with police as crowds expressed anger at the accused killer.

Crime & Justice.headline: Superdry co-founder James Holder convicted of rape after a night of drinking
Crime & Justice.detail: James Holder, the co-founder of Superdry, has been convicted of rape in England following accusations related to an incident after a night of drinking.

Crime & Justice.headline: Homicide detectives charge woman with allegedly perverting the course of justice in high-profile double murder trial
Crime & Justice.detail: NSW Police Homicide Squad detectives have charged a woman with allegedly perverting the course of justice in relation to an ongoing double murder trial.

Crime & Justice.headline: UK police charge man over stabbing of two Jewish men in London
Crime & Justice.detail: British police have charged a man in connection with the stabbing of two Jewish men in the Golders Green area of London, a neighborhood with a sizable Jewish population; the incident is being treated as a terrorist attack.

Education.headline: U.S. Department of Education Finalizes Landmark Rule to Lower College Costs and Simplify Student Loan Repayment
Education.detail: The U.S. Department of Education released a final rule that will lower the cost of college and make student loan repayment easier, which is a step toward implementing reforms in President Trump's Working Families Tax Cuts Act. The Department of Education also launched a new, real-time fraud detection capability for the Free Application for Federal Student Aid form, marking the largest and most comprehensive, nationwide fraud prevention effort in the agency's history.

Education.headline: ICSE, ISC results: Girls outperform boys, pass rate over 99%
Education.detail: The Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE) declared the ICSE (Class 10) and ISC (Class 12) 2026 results, with an overall pass percentage above 99% and girls outperforming boys. Over 3.6 lakh students appeared in the exams.

Education.headline: Modern education must for younger generation: IG FC
Education.detail: Inspector General Frontier Corps Balochistan (North), Major General Atif Mujtaba, said that modern education is essential for the younger generation to meet future challenges, and institutions like Cadet College Pishin are playing a pivotal role in shaping the country's future leadership. He stressed that cadet colleges and other educational institutions are not only imparting academic knowledge but also grooming students into responsible citizens.

Education.headline: SANU aligns with national skills agenda
Education.detail: The Southern Africa Nazarene University (SANU) held its 16th graduation ceremony, emphasizing the government's push for skills development, employability, and innovation as key drivers of Eswatini's economic transformation. Minister for Labour and Social Security Phila Buthelezi urged graduates to contribute to national development in a labor market that demands adaptability and creativity.

Education.headline: Three students. Lots of offers. Which college should they choose?
Education.detail: The Washington Post asked more than a dozen admissions experts what's most important when choosing a college and presented the scenarios of three students with multiple offers. The article explores the factors that students should consider when making their college decisions.

Lifestyle.headline: Annabel Crabb: 'I worried that people might think I'm an idiot'
Lifestyle.detail: The Australian journalist and TV presenter discusses her career and personal life in a recent interview.

Lifestyle.headline: Rebel Wilson's courtroom makeover shows why style matters on the stand
Lifestyle.detail: An analysis of Rebel Wilson's fashion choices during her recent court appearance and how it influenced public perception.

Lifestyle.headline: Ferragamo expands leather mapping efforts as EU sustainability rules take shape
Lifestyle.detail: The luxury brand is increasing its efforts to trace the origins of its leather as new EU regulations on sustainability are being developed.

Lifestyle.headline: Meet the couple who quit banking and finance to run tours of Singapore's abandoned places
Lifestyle.detail: Amanda Cheong and Stanley Cheah left their stable jobs to pursue their passion for history and now run a successful tour business.

Lifestyle.headline: Build-A-Bear recalls 36,000 plush bears over choking hazard from detachable zipper slider on pouch
Lifestyle.detail: Build-A-Bear Workshop is recalling approximately 36,000 plush bears in the U.S. due to a choking hazard.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Justice falling short on digital abuse
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A police officer in Kaohsiung was indicted for taking indecent photographs and using AI to create pornographic content, highlighting a broader issue of digital sexual abuse in Taiwan where complaints have risen significantly.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Debate must not become delay
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Taiwan's democratic success, built on pluralism, is facing a challenge as disagreement is increasingly used as a justification for political and institutional inaction, hindering progress on important issues like defense and constitutional matters.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Pollster competition is healthy
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The existence of multiple surveys done by non-colluding agents gives a basis for verification of a measurement, promoting healthy competition among pollsters.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Water vision
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Water insecurity in Pakistan has been building up for decades as per capita water availability has declined.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Letters to The Editor — May 2, 2026
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The U.S. administration’s claim that the war on Iran has already ended is one that would perhaps allow the White House to circumvent the requirement of Congressional approval under the War Powers Resolution, which constrains the President's military powers.

World.headline: China drops tariffs on all African nations except Eswatini
World.detail: China has dropped tariffs on all African nations except Eswatini, demonstrating a move towards stronger economic ties. This decision aims to bolster trade relations between China and the African continent.

World.headline: Rebel alliance seizes key Tessalit military camp in northern Mali
World.detail: A rebel alliance has taken control of a key military camp in Tessalit, located in northern Mali. This seizure marks a significant development in the ongoing conflict in the region.

World.headline: Kenya temporarily waives fuel standards to combat Iran war shortages
World.detail: To address shortages caused by the Iran war, Kenya has temporarily waived fuel standards. This measure is intended to ensure a stable supply of fuel during the crisis.

World.headline: U.S. to withdraw 5K troops from Germany, Pentagon says amid Trump-Merz feud
World.detail: The U.S. is set to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany, according to the Pentagon, amidst a feud between Trump and Merz. This decision follows criticism from Chancellor Friedrich Merz regarding the U.S. strategy in the war against Iran.

World.headline: Trump says he'll raise EU auto tariffs to 25% next week over trade dispute
World.detail: President Trump announced he will raise tariffs on EU automobiles to 25% next week due to an ongoing trade dispute. The President claims the EU is not complying with the current trade deal.

Zimbabwe.headline: Dam surges spark productivity boom
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe's 154 major dams are now averaging over 90 percent capacity, significantly boosting prospects for agriculture, industry, and power generation.

Zimbabwe.headline: Perfect storm of 'adverse factors' . . . as double whammy of price hikes and power cuts batters Zimbabwe
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is facing a challenging economic environment due to price hikes and power cuts.

Zimbabwe.headline: Midlands car theft syndicate smashed
Zimbabwe.detail: A car theft syndicate operating in the Midlands province has been dismantled.

Zimbabwe.headline: Court rules against RBZ in forex case
Zimbabwe.detail: The High Court has ruled against the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe in a foreign exchange case.

Zimbabwe.headline: Harare defaulters face eviction, property seizure
Zimbabwe.detail: Residents of Harare who have defaulted on their payments to the city council may face eviction and property seizure.
